prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
1. *"Lo! In this third quarter of the Year of Our Lord Two Thousand and Twenty-Four, the sorcerers of ‘Salesforce’ did summon forth a great host of ‘recurring revenues,’ as though conjuring gold from the very aether—yet their ‘churn rate’ did rise like a plague upon the land, and none could say whether this was the work of demons or merely poor onboarding."*

2. *"The High Priests of ‘Customer Success’ did gather in their glass-and-steel sanctum, muttering of ‘ARR’ and ‘MRR’ as though they were sacred runes, while the lowly scribes toiled in the ‘Slack’—a cursed mirror-realm where messages appear and vanish like ghosts, and no man may find repose."*

3. *"Behold! The ‘CEO,’ a man of strange and shifting titles, did proclaim a ‘pivot to AI,’ though none could fathom what manner of beast this ‘AI’ might be—some said it was a golem of pure logic, others whispered it was but a very clever parrot trained to speak in riddles about ‘synergies.’"*

4. *"And thus did the quarter close with ‘beat expectations,’ a phrase of great mystery, for who sets these ‘expectations,’ and by what dark arts are they divined? The ledgers show profit, yet the common folk grow restless, for their ‘401(k)s’ remain as inscrutable as the movements of the stars."*
